summary.para: By default, the library searches all fonts available in the runtime environment.
This may work well on the development environment but may fail in the cloud where fonts are usually not installed.
It is safest deploy font files along with the application and then register them using this class.

member.summary: Returns the total count of pages in the document.

remarks.para: Document rendering process is performed in two phases. During the first phase, the value of this property is equal to int.MaxValue to indicate its unavailability.
Please note that using this property may result with unstable layouts and unpredicted behaviors, especially when generating conditional content of various sizes.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Elements.MultiColumn.OptimizeTextCacheBehavior
member.summary: The TextBlock element uses SkParagraph cache to enhance rendering speed. This cache uses a significant amount of memory and is cleared after FullRender. However, the MultiColumn element uses a sophisticated measuring algorithm, and may force the Text element to measure/render multiple times per page. To avoid performance issues, the TextBlock element should keep its cache.

summary.para: The TextBlock element uses SkParagraph cache to enhance rendering speed. This cache uses a significant amount of memory and is cleared after FullRender. However, when using the RepeatContent element, the cache is cleared after each repetition. To avoid performance issues, the default behavior is disabled.

Similarly, the Lazy element builds entire content on demand, waits to fully render it and then removes it. This aims to optimize managed memory usage. However, it may not be the most optimal solution in repeating context.

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Elements.ShowIfContext.TotalPages
member.summary: Returns the total count of pages in the document.

remarks.para: Document rendering process is performed in two phases. During the first phase, the value of this property is equal to null to indicate its unavailability.
Please note that using this property may result with unstable layouts and unpredicted behaviors, especially when generating conditional content of various sizes.

member.name: T:QuestPDF.Elements.Table.DynamicDictionary`2
member.summary: This dictionary allows to access key that does not exist. Instead of throwing an exception, it returns a default value.

member.name: F:QuestPDF.Elements.Text.TextBlock.PlanLayoutLock
member.summary:
Adjusts the concurrency level for the SkParagraph.PlanLayout method to optimize performance.
While the Skia implementation is thread-safe, it appears to contain internal locks that hinder scalability. Consequently, using multithreading for document rendering can reduce performance. This includes increased memory usage and slower generation times—potentially even worse than rendering documents sequentially.
TODO: investigate further on how to improve scalability and remove this mutex

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.ComponentExtensions.Component``1(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,``0)
member.summary: Components serve as modular building blocks for abstracting document layouts. They promote code reusability across multiple sections or types of documents. Using a component, you can generate content based on its internal state. Learn more
member.example: Consider the scenario of a company-wide page header. Instead of replicating the same header design across various documents, a single component can be created and referenced wherever needed.
member.param: Instance of the class implementing the interface.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.ContentDirectionExtensions.ContentFromLeftToRight(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Sets the left-to-right (LTR) direction for its entire content. Learn more

remarks.para: This writing system is used by most of modern languages.

The content direction affects various layout structures. In LTR mode, items are typically aligned to the left. This mode also influences the direction of items in certain layouts. For instance, in a row element with LTR mode, the first item is positioned on the left, while the last item is on the right.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.ContentDirectionExtensions.ContentFromRightToLeft(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Sets the right-to-left (RTL) direction for its entire content. Learn more

remarks.para: This writing system is used by languages such as Hebrew, Arabic, and Persian.

The content direction affects various layout structures. In RTL mode, items are typically aligned to the right. This mode also influences the direction of items in certain layouts. For instance, in a row element with RTL mode, the first item is positioned on the right, while the last item is on the left.

Draws a labeled box around its inner content. Useful for visual debugging and pinpointing output from specific code blocks.

member.param: Optional label displayed within the box.
Color in a valid format (like #FF8800 for orange) or from the predefined set (like Colors.Red.Lighten1).

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.DebugExtensions.DebugPointer(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.String)

summary.para: Inserts a virtual debug element visible in the document hierarchy tree in the QuestPDF Companion App, as well as in the enhanced debugging message provided by the .
It helps with understanding and navigation of the document hierarchy.

member.remarks: This debug element does not appear in the final PDF output.
member.param: Text visible somewhere in the "element trace" content identifying given document fragment.

member.remarks: This container is fully visible on each page and does not support paging.

member.remarks: This container does support paging.

member.remarks: This container is fully visible on each page and does not support paging.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.DecorationDescriptor.After(System.Action{QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer})

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.DecorationExtensions.Decoration(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.Action{QuestPDF.Fluent.DecorationDescriptor})

summary.para: Divides the container's space into three distinct sections: before, content, and after.
The "before" section is rendered above the main content, while the "after" section is rendered below it.
If the main "content" spans across multiple pages, both the "before" and "after" sections are consistently rendered on every page.

member.example: A typical use-case for this method is to render a table that spans multiple pages, with a consistent caption or header on each page.
member.param: The action to configure the content.

member.name: T:QuestPDF.Fluent.DocumentOperation
member.summary: Provides functionality for performing various operations on PDF documents, including loading, merging, overlaying, underlaying, selecting specific pages, adding attachments, and applying encryption settings.

member.name: T:QuestPDF.Fluent.DocumentOperation.LayerConfiguration
member.summary: Represents configuration options for applying an overlay or underlay to a PDF document using qpdf.

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Fluent.DocumentOperation.LayerConfiguration.FilePath
member.summary: The file path of the overlay or underlay PDF file to be used.

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Fluent.DocumentOperation.LayerConfiguration.TargetPages
member.summary: Specifies the range of pages in the output document where the overlay or underlay will be applied. If not specified, the overlay or underlay is applied to all output pages.

item.description: Plain numbers indicate pages numbered from the start
Numbers with 'r' prefix count from the end (r1 = last page)
Represents the last page (equivalent to r1)
Dash-separated ranges are inclusive
Reversed ranges list pages in descending order
Excludes specified pages from previous range
Selects odd-positioned pages from the resulting page-set
Selects even-positioned pages from the resulting page-set
pages 1, 6, and 4 in that order
pages 3 through 7 inclusive
pages 7, 6, 5, 4, and 3 in that order
all pages in order
all pages in reverse order
the last three pages of the document
the last three pages of the document in reverse order
even pages from 2 to 20
pages 5, 7, 8, 9, and 12
pages 5, 8, and 12 (pages in odd positions from the original set of 5, 7, 8, 9, 12)
pages 7 and 9 (pages in even positions from the original set of 5, 7, 8, 9, 12)
pages 1 through 10 except pages 3 and 4 (1, 2, and 5 through 10)
In a 15-page file: pages 4, 5, 6, 10, 12, 10, 9, and 8 in that order (pages 4 through 10 except 7 through 9, followed by 12 through 8 descending, except 11 which is the fifth page from the end)

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Fluent.DocumentOperation.DocumentAttachment.Description

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Fluent.DocumentOperation.DocumentAttachment.Replace
member.summary: Indicates whether to replace an existing attachment with the same key. If false, an exception is thrown if an attachment with the same key already exists.

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Fluent.DocumentOperation.DocumentAttachment.Relationship
member.summary: Specifies the relationship of the embedded file to the document for PDF/A-3b compliance.

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Fluent.DocumentOperation.EncryptionBase.UserPassword
member.summary: The user password for the PDF, allowing restricted access based on encryption settings. May be left null to enable opening the PDF without a password, though this may restrict certain operations.

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Fluent.DocumentOperation.EncryptionBase.OwnerPassword
member.summary: The owner password for the PDF, granting full access to all document features. An empty owner password is considered insecure, as is using the same value for both user and owner passwords.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.DocumentOperation.UnderlayFile(QuestPDF.Fluent.DocumentOperation.LayerConfiguration)
member.summary: Applies an underlay to the document using the specified configuration. The underlay pages are drawn beneath the target pages in the output file, potentially obscured by the original content.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.DocumentOperation.OverlayFile(QuestPDF.Fluent.DocumentOperation.LayerConfiguration)
member.summary: Applies an overlay to the document using the specified configuration. The overlay pages are drawn on top of the target pages in the output file, potentially obscuring the original content.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.DocumentOperation.ExtendMetadata(System.String)
member.summary: Extends the current document's XMP metadata by adding content within the rdf:Description tag. This allows for adding additional descriptive metadata to the PDF, which is useful for compliance standards like PDF/A or for industry-specific metadata (e.g., ZUGFeRD).
member.param: A string containing the metadata to add. This metadata must be valid XML content and conform to the RDF structure required by the PDF XMP metadata specification.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.DocumentOperation.AddAttachment(QuestPDF.Fluent.DocumentOperation.DocumentAttachment)
member.summary: Adds an attachment to the document, with specified metadata and configuration options.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.DocumentOperation.Decrypt
member.summary: Removes any existing encryption from the current PDF document, effectively making it accessible without a password or encryption restrictions.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.DocumentOperation.RemoveRestrictions
member.summary: Remove security restrictions associated with digitally signed PDF files. This may be combined with Decrypt() operation to allow free editing of previously signed/encrypted files. This option invalidates and disables any digital signatures but leaves their visual appearances intact.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.DocumentOperation.Encrypt(QuestPDF.Fluent.DocumentOperation.Encryption40Bit)
member.summary: Encrypts the document using 40-bit encryption, applying specified owner and user passwords along with defined permissions.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.DocumentOperation.Encrypt(QuestPDF.Fluent.DocumentOperation.Encryption128Bit)
member.summary: Encrypts the document using 128-bit encryption, applying specified owner and user passwords along with defined permissions.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.DocumentOperation.Encrypt(QuestPDF.Fluent.DocumentOperation.Encryption256Bit)
member.summary: Encrypts the document using 256-bit encryption, applying specified owner and user passwords along with defined permissions.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.DocumentOperation.Linearize
member.summary: Creates linearized (web-optimized) output files. Linearized files are structured to allow compliant PDF readers to begin displaying content before the entire file is downloaded. Normally, a PDF reader requires the entire file to be present to render content, as essential cross-reference data typically appears at the file’s end.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.DocumentOperation.Save(System.String)
member.summary: Executes the configured operations on the document and saves the resulting file to the specified path.
member.param: The path where the output file will be saved.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.DynamicComponentExtensions.Dynamic(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IDynamicComponent)
member.summary: Represents a dynamically generated section of the document. Components are page-aware, understand their positioning, can dynamically construct other content elements, and assess their dimensions, enabling complex layout creations. Learn more

example.para: Consider an invoice that presents all purchased items in a table format. Instead of just showing the final total price under the table, the requirement is to display the cumulative prices on each separate page.

Using the dynamic component, you can manually assemble the table, count how many items are visible on each page, calculate the price sum for items visible on each page, and then render the result under each sub-table.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.DynamicComponentExtensions.Dynamic``1(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IDynamicComponent{``0})
member.summary: Represents a section of the document dynamically created based on its inner state. Components are page-aware, understand their positioning, can dynamically construct other content elements, and assess their dimensions, enabling complex layout creations. Learn more

example.para: Consider an invoice that presents all purchased items in a table format. Instead of just showing the final total price under the table, the requirement is to display the cumulative prices on each separate page.

Using the dynamic component, you can manually assemble the table, count how many items are visible on each page, calculate the price sum for items visible on each page, and then render the result under each sub-table.

member.summary: Passes the Fluent API chain to the provided method. Learn more

remarks.para: This method is particularly useful for code refactoring, improving its structure and readability.
Extracting implementation of certain layout structures into separate methods, allows you to accurately describe their purpose and reuse them code in various parts of the application.

member.param: A delegate that takes the current container and populates it with content.

Extracting implementation of certain layout structures into separate methods, allows you to accurately describe their purpose and reuse them code in various parts of the application.

member.param: A method that accepts the current container, optionally populates it with content, and returns a subsequent container to continue the Fluent API chain.

member.summary: Constrains its content to maintain a given width-to-height ratio. Learn more

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.ElementExtensions.Placeholder(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.String)
member.summary: Draws a basic placeholder useful for prototyping. Learn more
member.remarks:
You can control the size of the Placeholder by chaining other elements before its invocation, e.g.:
.Width(200) .Height(100) .Placeholder("Sample text");
member.param: When provided, the placeholder displays this text. If omitted, a simple image icon is shown instead.

summary.para: The ShowOnce element modifies how content is displayed across multiple pages.

By default, all elements are fully rendered only once and never repeated. However, in some contexts such as page headers and footers, as well as decoration before and after slots, the content is repeated on every page. To prevent this, you can use the ShowOnce element.

example.para: Combine this element with SkipOnce to achieve more complex behaviors, e.g.:
.SkipOnce().ShowOnce() ensures the child element is displayed only on the second page.
.SkipOnce().SkipOnce() starts displaying the child element from the third page onwards.
.ShowOnce().SkipOnce() draws nothing, as the order of invocation is important.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.ElementExtensions.SkipOnce(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: If the container spans multiple pages, its content is omitted on the first page and then displayed on the second and subsequent pages. Learn more
member.remarks: A common use-case for this element is when displaying a consistent header across pages but needing to conditionally show/hide specific fragments on the first page.

example.para: Combine this element with ShowOnce to achieve more complex behaviors, e.g.:
.SkipOnce().ShowOnce() ensures the child element is displayed only on the second page.
.SkipOnce().SkipOnce() starts displaying the child element from the third page onwards.
.ShowOnce().SkipOnce() draws nothing, as the order of invocation is important.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.ElementExtensions.ShowEntire(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Ensures its content is displayed entirely on a single page by disabling the default paging capability. Learn more

remarks.para: While many library elements inherently support paging, allowing content to span multiple pages, this element restricts that behavior.
Employ this when a single-page display is crucial.
Be cautious: its strict space constraints can trigger the if content exceeds the page's capacity.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.ElementExtensions.EnsureSpace(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.Single)

summary.para: Ensures that the container's content occupies at least a specified minimum height on its first page of occurrence. If there is enough space, the content is rendered as usual. However, if a page break is required, this method ensures that a minimum amount of space is available before rendering the content. If the required space is not available, the content is moved to the next page.
This rule applies only to the first page where the content appears. If the content spans multiple pages, all subsequent pages are rendered without this restriction.

This method is particularly useful for structured elements like tables, where rendering only a small fragment at the bottom of a page could negatively impact readability. By ensuring a minimum height, you can prevent undesired content fragmentation.

member.param: The minimum height, in points, that the element should occupy before a page break.

summary.para: Attempts to keep the container's content together on its first page of occurrence. If the content does not fit entirely on that page, it is moved to the next page. If it spans multiple pages, all subsequent pages are rendered as usual without restriction.
This method is useful for ensuring that content remains visually coherent and is not arbitrarily split.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.ElementExtensions.PageBreak(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Inserts a break that pushes the subsequent content to start on a new page. Learn more

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.ElementExtensions.Container(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: A neutral layout structure that neither contributes to nor alters its content.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.ElementExtensions.Repeat(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Repeats its content across multiple pages. Learn more
member.remarks: In certain layout structures, the content visibility may depend on other elements. By default, most elements are rendered only once. Use this element to repeat the content across multiple pages.

summary.para: Delays the creation of document content and reduces its lifetime, significantly lowering memory usage in large documents containing thousands of pages. This approach also enhances garbage collection efficiency in memory-constrained environments.

The provided delegate is invoked later in the document generation process. For optimal performance, divide your document into smaller sections, each encapsulated within its own Lazy element. Further optimizations can be achieved by nesting Lazy elements within each other. However, note that this technique may increase the overall document generation time due to deferred content processing.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.ElementExtensions.LazyWithCache(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.Action{QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer})
member.summary: Functions similarly to the Lazy element but enables the library to use caching mechanisms for the content. This can help optimize managed memory usage, although native memory usage may remain high. Use LazyWithCache only when the increased generation time associated with the Lazy element is unacceptable. Learn more

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.ElementExtensions.ZIndex(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.Int32)
member.summary: By default, the library draws content in the order it is defined, which may not always be the desired behavior. This element allows you to alter the rendering order, ensuring that the content is displayed in the correct sequence. The default z-index is 0, unless a different value is inherited from a parent container. Learn more
member.param: The z-index value. Higher values are rendered above lower values.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.DynamicImageDescriptor.UseOriginalImage(System.Boolean)
member.summary: When enabled, the library does not resize the image to achieve the target DPI, nor compress it with target image quality.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.DynamicImageDescriptor.WithRasterDpi(System.Int32)

summary.para: Specifies the DPI (dots-per-inch) for rasterizing images, which is a measure of an image's resolution that indicates how many individual dots (or pixels) fit into one inch of a printed image. Higher DPI values result in greater detail and sharpness, making images appear clearer when printed, while lower DPI values can cause images to look blurry or pixelated.
The target resolution is computed by multiplying the DPI with the physical image size on the document.
Default DPI value is 288 DPI.

member.remarks: If the image has lower resolution that the one calculated from the DPI setting, it will NOT be rescaled.
member.example: Consider an image of dimensions 3x4 inches. Using a DPI value of 300, the final resolution translates to 900x1200 pixels.

summary.para: Controls the balance between an image's file size and its visual fidelity during compression. Higher quality values preserve more detail with larger file sizes, while lower values reduce file size at the cost of potential image degradation, such as blurriness or artifacts.
Opaque images are JPEG-encoded based on this setting, while images with an alpha channel default to PNG format, disregarding this option.
Default is set to "high quality".

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.ImageDescriptor.UseOriginalImage(System.Boolean)
member.summary: When enabled, the library does not resize the image to achieve the target DPI, nor compress it with target image quality.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.ImageDescriptor.WithRasterDpi(System.Int32)

summary.para: Specifies the DPI (dots-per-inch) for rasterizing images, which is a measure of an image's resolution that indicates how many individual dots (or pixels) fit into one inch of a printed image. Higher DPI values result in greater detail and sharpness, making images appear clearer when printed, while lower DPI values can cause images to look blurry or pixelated.
The target resolution is computed by multiplying the DPI with the physical image size on the document.
Default DPI value is 288 DPI.

member.remarks: If the image has lower resolution that the one calculated from the DPI setting, it will NOT be rescaled.
member.example: Consider an image of dimensions 3x4 inches. Using a DPI value of 300, the final resolution translates to 900x1200 pixels.

summary.para: Controls the balance between an image's file size and its visual fidelity during compression. Higher quality values preserve more detail with larger file sizes, while lower values reduce file size at the cost of potential image degradation, such as blurriness or artifacts.
Opaque images are JPEG-encoded based on this setting, while images with an alpha channel default to PNG format, disregarding this option.
Default is set to "high quality".

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.ImageDescriptor.FitWidth
member.summary: Scales the image to fill the full width of its container. This is the default behavior.

summary.para: The image stretches vertically to fit the full available height.
Often used with height-constraining elements such as: Height, MaxHeight, etc.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.ImageDescriptor.FitArea
member.summary: Combines the FitWidth and FitHeight settings. The image resizes itself to utilize all available space, preserving its aspect ratio. It will either fill the width or height based on the container's dimensions.
member.remarks: An optimal and safe choice.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.ImageDescriptor.FitUnproportionally
member.summary: The image adjusts to fill all the available space, disregarding its original proportions. This can lead to distorted scaling and is generally not recommended for most scenarios.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.ImageExtensions.Image(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.Byte[])
member.summary: Draws an image by decoding it from a provided byte array. Learn more

remarks.para: Please note that there is a significant difference between image resolution (number of pixels vertically and horizontally) and its physical size described in points. Therefore, the resolution of an image is not used for determining its physical size on the document.

Multiple strategies exist for the Image element to determine its final size. By default, the image fills all the available width. This behavior can be customized using options within the descriptor class.
Images are automatically resized and compressed based on the descriptor's setup. It defaults to global document's settings.
Supported formats: PNG, JPEG and WEBP.

member.returns: Descriptor allowing adjustments to image attributes, such as scaling behavior, compression quality, and target DPI.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.LayersDescriptor.Layer
member.summary: Specifies an additional layer for the container.

remarks.para: The order of code execution determines the drawing order:
If the layer is defined before the primary layer, it's drawn underneath the primary content (as a background).
If defined after the primary layer, it's drawn in front of the primary content (as a watermark).

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.LayersDescriptor.PrimaryLayer
member.summary: Sets the primary content for the container.
member.remarks: Exactly one primary layer should be defined.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.LayerExtensions.Layers(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.Action{QuestPDF.Fluent.LayersDescriptor})

summary.para: Adds content either underneath (as a background) or on top of (as a watermark) the main content.
The main layer supports paging, can span multiple pages, and determines the container's target length.
Additional layers can also span multiple pages and are repeated on each one.

member.param: Handler for defining content of the container, including exactly one primary layer and any additional layers in a specified order.

member.summary: Configures the metadata of the PDF document, such as title, author, keywords, etc.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.Document.WithSettings(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.DocumentSettings)
member.summary: Enables fine-tuning of the document generation process, influencing attributes of the resulting PDF such as target DPI, image compression, compliance with the PDF/A standard, etc.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.Document.Merge(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IDocument})
member.summary: Combines multiple documents together into a single one.
member.returns: A MergedDocument object that allows to set metadata, configure generation parameters, adjust merging strategy, and produce output files such as PDF, XPS, or images.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.Document.Merge(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IDocument[])
member.summary: Combines multiple documents together into a single one.
member.returns: A MergedDocument object that allows to set metadata, configure generation parameters, adjust merging strategy, and produce output files such as PDF, XPS, or images.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.Document.GetMetadata
member.summary: Implements the IDocument interface. Don't use within the Fluent API chain.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.Document.GetSettings
member.summary: Implements the IDocument interface. Don't use within the Fluent API chain.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.Document.Compose(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IDocumentContainer)
member.summary: Implements the IDocument interface. Don't use within the Fluent API chain.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.MultiColumnDescriptor.Spacing(System.Single,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Unit)
member.summary: Configures the horizontal spacing between adjacent columns in the layout.
member.remarks: This method affects the visual presentation of the column arrangement. Positive values increase separation, while negative values may cause overlap.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.MultiColumnDescriptor.Columns(System.Int32)
member.summary: Defines the number of vertical columns in the layout.
member.remarks: This method determines the basic structure of the grid layout. Setting this value will redistribute the existing elements across the new column count.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.MultiColumnDescriptor.BalanceHeight(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Controls the content distribution across columns to achieve balanced heights.

remarks.para: When enabled: content flow is adjusted to equalize column heights; each column will have approximately the same height.
When disabled: layout occupies the full vertical space, the last column may be shorter or empty, depending on content quantity.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.MultiColumnDescriptor.Content
member.summary: Retrieves a container for the primary content to be distributed across multiple columns.
member.remarks: The returned container serves as the main content area for the multi-column layout. It supports all available layout elements and automatically divides its content among the defined columns.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.MultiColumnDescriptor.Spacer
member.summary: Retrieves a container for content positioned between columns in the layout.
member.remarks: The container's dimensions are determined by the height of the columns and the configured spacing. This container supports all available layout elements, allowing for flexible design of inter-column content.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.MultiColumnExtensions.MultiColumn(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.Action{QuestPDF.Fluent.MultiColumnDescriptor})
member.summary: Creates a multi-column layout within the current container element.
member.remarks: A multi-column layout organizes content into vertical columns, similar to a newspaper or magazine layout. This approach allows for efficient use of horizontal space and can improve readability, especially for wide containers or screens. The content flows from one column to the next, and the number of columns can be adjusted based on the container's width or specific design requirements.
member.param: The action to configure the column's content and behavior.

summary.para: For positive values, adds empty space around its content.
For negative values, pushes its content beyond the edges, increasing available space, similarly to negative HTML margins.
For positive values, adds empty space horizontally (left and right) around its content.
For negative values, pushes its content beyond the horizontal edges, increasing available space, similarly to negative HTML margins.
For positive values, adds empty space vertically (top and bottom) around its content.
For negative values, pushes its content beyond the vertical edges, increasing available space, similarly to negative HTML margins.
For positive values, adds empty space above its content.
For negative values, pushes its content beyond the top edge, increasing available space, similarly to negative HTML margins.
For positive values, adds empty space below its content.
For negative values, pushes its content beyond the bottom edge, increasing available space, similarly to negative HTML margins.
For positive values, adds empty space to the left of its content.
For negative values, pushes its content beyond the left edge, increasing available space, similarly to negative HTML margins.
For positive values, adds empty space to the right of its content.
For negative values, pushes its content beyond the right edge, increasing available space, similarly to negative HTML margins.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.PageDescriptor.Size(System.Single,System.Single,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Unit)
member.summary: Configures the dimensions of every page within the set.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.PageDescriptor.Size(QuestPDF.Helpers.PageSize)
member.summary: Configures the dimensions of every page within the set.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.PageDescriptor.ContinuousSize(System.Single,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Unit)
member.summary: Enables the continuous page size mode, allowing the page's height to adjust according to content while retaining a constant specified .
member.example: This configuration is suitable for producing PDFs intended for printing on the paper roll.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.PageDescriptor.MinSize(QuestPDF.Helpers.PageSize)
member.summary: Enables the flexible page size mode, where the output page's dimensions can vary based on its content. Specifies the smallest possible page size.
member.remarks: Note that with this setting, individual pages within the document may have different sizes.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.PageDescriptor.MaxSize(QuestPDF.Helpers.PageSize)
member.summary: Enables the flexible page size mode, where the output page's dimensions can vary based on its content. Specifies the largest possible page size.
member.remarks: Note that with this setting, individual pages within the document may have different sizes.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.PageDescriptor.MarginLeft(System.Single,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Unit)
member.summary: Adds empty space to the left of the primary layer (header + content + footer).

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.PageDescriptor.MarginRight(System.Single,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Unit)
member.summary: Adds empty space to the right of the primary layer (header + content + footer).

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.PageDescriptor.MarginTop(System.Single,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Unit)
member.summary: Adds empty space above the primary layer (header + content + footer).

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.PageDescriptor.MarginBottom(System.Single,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Unit)
member.summary: Adds empty space below the primary layer (header + content + footer).

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.PageDescriptor.MarginVertical(System.Single,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Unit)
member.summary: Adds empty space vertically (top and bottom) around the primary layer (header + content + footer).

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.PageDescriptor.MarginHorizontal(System.Single,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Unit)
member.summary: Adds empty space horizontally (left and right) around the primary layer (header + content + footer).

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.PageDescriptor.Margin(System.Single,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Unit)
member.summary: Adds empty space around the primary layer (header + content + footer).

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.PageDescriptor.DefaultTextStyle(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.TextStyle)
member.summary: Applies a default text style to all Text elements within the page set.
member.remarks: Use this method to achieve consistent text styling across entire document.
member.param: A delegate to adjust the global text style attributes.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.PageDescriptor.DefaultTextStyle(System.Func{QuestPDF.Infrastructure.TextStyle,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.TextStyle})
member.summary: Applies a default text style to all Text elements within the page set.
member.remarks: Use this method to achieve consistent text styling across entire document.
member.param: A handler to adjust the global text style attributes.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.PageDescriptor.ContentFromLeftToRight
member.summary: Applies a left-to-right (LTR) content direction to all elements within the page set. Learn more

remarks.para: This writing system is used by most of modern languages.

The content direction affects various layout structures. In LTR mode, items are typically aligned to the left. This mode also influences the direction of items in certain layouts. For instance, in a row element with LTR mode, the first item is positioned on the left, while the last item is on the right.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.PageDescriptor.ContentFromRightToLeft
member.summary: Applies a right-to-left (RTL) content direction to all elements within the page set. Learn more

remarks.para: This writing system is used by languages such as Hebrew, Arabic, and Persian.

The content direction affects various layout structures. In RTL mode, items are typically aligned to the right. This mode also influences the direction of items in certain layouts. For instance, in a row element with RTL mode, the first item is positioned on the right, while the last item is on the left.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.PageDescriptor.PageColor(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Color)
member.summary: Sets a background color of the page, which is white by default.
member.remarks: When working with file formats that support the alpha channel, it is possible to set the color to if necessary.
member.param: Color in a valid format (like #FF8800 for orange) or from the predefined set (like Colors.Red.Lighten1).

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.PageDescriptor.Background
member.summary: Represents a layer drawn behind the primary layer (header + content + footer), serving as a background.
member.remarks: Unaffected by the Margin configuration, it always occupies entire page.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.PageDescriptor.Foreground
member.summary: Represents a layer drawn in front of the primary layer (header + content + footer), serving as a watermark.
member.remarks: Unaffected by the Margin configuration, it always occupies entire page.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.PageDescriptor.Header
member.summary: Represents the segment at the very top of the page, just above the main content.
member.remarks: This container does not support paging capability. It's expected to be fully displayed on every page.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.PageDescriptor.Content
member.summary: Represents the primary content, located in-between the header and footer.
member.remarks: This container does support paging capability and determines the final lenght of the document.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.PageDescriptor.Footer
member.summary: Represents the section at the very bottom of the page, just below the main content.
member.remarks: This container does not support paging capability. It's expected to be fully displayed on every page.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.PageExtensions.Page(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IDocumentContainer,System.Action{QuestPDF.Fluent.PageDescriptor})
member.summary: Creates a new page set with consistent attributes, such as margin, color, and watermark. The length of each set depends on its content.
member.remarks: By leveraging multiple page sets, you can produce documents containing pages of distinct sizes and characteristics.
member.param: Delegate to define page content (layout and visual elements).
member.returns: Continuation of the Document API chain, permitting the definition of other page sets.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.RotateExtensions.RotateLeft(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Rotates its content 90 degrees counterclockwise. Learn more
member.remarks: Note: Rotation can alter certain attributes; for example, 'width' might effectively become 'height'.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.RotateExtensions.RotateRight(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Rotates its content 90 degrees clockwise. Learn more
member.remarks: Note: Rotation can alter certain attributes; for example, 'width' might effectively become 'height'.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.RotateExtensions.Rotate(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.Single)
member.summary: Rotates its content clockwise by a given angle. Learn more
member.param: Rotation angle in degrees. A value of 360 degrees represents a full rotation.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.RowDescriptor.Spacing(System.Single,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Unit)
member.summary: Adjusts horizontal spacing between items.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.RowDescriptor.RelativeItem(System.Single)
member.summary: Adds a new item to the row element. This item occupies space proportionally to other relative items.
member.example: For a row element with a width of 100 points that has three items (a relative item of size 1, a relative item of size 5, and a constant item of size 10 points), the items will occupy sizes of 15 points, 75 points, and 10 points respectively.
member.returns: The container of the newly added item.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.RowDescriptor.ConstantItem(System.Single,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Unit)
member.summary: Adds a new item to the row element with a specified constant size.
member.returns: The container of the newly created item.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.RowDescriptor.AutoItem
member.summary: Adds a new item to the row element. The size of this item adjusts based on its content.

remarks.para: The AutoItem requests as much horizontal space as its content requires.
It doesn't adjust its size based on other items and may frequently result in a .
It's recommended to use this API in conjunction with the MaxWidth element.

member.returns: The container of the newly created item.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.RowExtensions.Row(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.Action{QuestPDF.Fluent.RowDescriptor})
member.summary:
Draws a collection of elements horizontally. Depending on the content direction mode, elements will be drawn from left to right, or from right to left.
Learn more

remarks.para: Supports paging.
Depending on its content, the Row element may repeatedly draw certain items across multiple pages. Use the ShowOnce element to modify this behavior if it's not desired.

member.param: The action to configure the row's content.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.ScaleExtensions.Scale(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.Single)
member.summary: Scales its inner content proportionally. Learn more

remarks.para: Although this adjustment modifies the space available to its inner content, some elements might use their own strategies to fill that space.
For example, an Image with the setting may retain its size, but its quality could vary based on the DPI setting.
In contrast, text will not only appear smaller or bigger; but also a different number of words may fit each line.

member.param: The scaling factor. Values greater than one enlarge the content, while values less than one reduce it.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.ScaleExtensions.ScaleHorizontal(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.Single)
member.summary: Scales the available horizontal space (along the X axis), causing content to appear expanded or squished, rather than simply larger or smaller. Learn more

remarks.para: Although this adjustment modifies the space available to its inner content, some elements might use their own strategies to fill that space.
For example, an Image with the setting may retain its size, but its quality could vary based on the DPI setting.
In contrast, text will not only appear smaller or bigger; but also a different number of words may fit each line.

member.param: The scaling factor. Values greater than one enlarge the content, while values less than one reduce it.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.ScaleExtensions.ScaleVertical(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.Single)
member.summary: Scales the available vertical space (along the Y axis), causing content to appear expanded or squished, rather than simply larger or smaller. Learn more

remarks.para: Although this adjustment modifies the space available to its inner content, some elements might use their own strategies to fill that space.
For example, an Image with the setting may retain its size, but its quality could vary based on the DPI setting.
In contrast, text will not only appear smaller or bigger; but also a different number of words may fit each line.

member.param: The scaling factor. Values greater than one enlarge the content, while values less than one reduce it.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.ScaleExtensions.FlipHorizontal(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Flips its content to create a mirror image along the Y axis, swapping elements from left to right. Learn more
member.example: Elements on the left will appear on the right.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.ScaleExtensions.FlipVertical(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Flips its content to create a mirror image along the X axis, moving elements from the top to the bottom. Learn more
member.example: Elements at the top will be positioned at the bottom.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.ScaleExtensions.FlipOver(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Creates a mirror image of its content across both axes. Learn more
member.example: Elements originally in the top-left corner will be positioned in the bottom-right corner.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SemanticExtensions.SemanticIgnore(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Excludes the container content from the semantic tree. Use for decorative elements, layout artifacts, or other non-structural content that shouldn't be part of the document's logical structure.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SemanticExtensions.SemanticArticle(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Marks a self-contained body of text that forms a single narrative or exposition, such as a blog post, news story, or forum post. As a best practice, articles should not be nested within each other.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SemanticExtensions.SemanticSection(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Applies a 'Section' tag, grouping a set of related content. A section typically includes a heading (e.g., SemanticHeader2) and its corresponding content. Sections can be nested to create a hierarchical document structure.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SemanticExtensions.SemanticDivision(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Marks a generic block-level container for grouping elements. It's often used when a more specific semantic tag (like 'Article' or 'Section') doesn't apply, serving as a general-purpose 'div', similar to its HTML counterpart.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SemanticExtensions.SemanticBlockQuotation(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Designates a block of text that is a quotation, typically consisting of one or more paragraphs. This is for block-level quotes, as opposed to which is for inline text.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SemanticExtensions.SemanticCaption(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Identifies a brief portion of text that serves as a caption or description for a table, figure, or image. It should be placed near the element it describes.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SemanticExtensions.SemanticIndex(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Marks a section of the document as an index. This container typically holds a sequence of entries and references.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SemanticExtensions.SemanticLanguage(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.String)
member.summary: Applies a language attribute to a container, specifying the natural language (e.g., 'en-US', 'es-ES') of its content. This is crucial for accessibility, enabling screen readers to use the correct pronunciation.
member.param: The ISO 639 language code (e.g., 'en-US' or 'fr-FR') for the content.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SemanticExtensions.SemanticTableOfContents(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary:
Marks a container as a Table of Contents (TOC).
A TOC should be composed of elements. TOCs can be nested to represent a hierarchical document structure.
This tag can also be used for lists of figures, lists of tables, or bibliographies.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SemanticExtensions.SemanticTableOfContentsItem(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Marks an individual item within a . This typically represents a single entry in the list.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SemanticExtensions.SemanticHeader1(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Marks the content as a level 1 heading (H1), the highest level in the document hierarchy. Headings are crucial for navigation and outlining the document's structure.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SemanticExtensions.SemanticHeader2(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Marks the content as a level 2 heading (H2).

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SemanticExtensions.SemanticHeader3(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Marks the content as a level 3 heading (H3).

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SemanticExtensions.SemanticHeader4(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Marks the content as a level 4 heading (H4).

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SemanticExtensions.SemanticHeader5(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Marks the content as a level 5 heading (H5).

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SemanticExtensions.SemanticHeader6(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Marks the content as a level 6 heading (H6), the lowest level in the document hierarchy.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SemanticExtensions.SemanticParagraph(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Marks a container as a paragraph. This is one of the most common block-level tags for organizing text content.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SemanticExtensions.SemanticList(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Marks a container as a list. Its direct children should be one or more elements. A can also be included as an optional first child.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SemanticExtensions.SemanticListItem(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Marks an individual item within a . Its children should typically be a (e.g., the bullet or number) and/or a (the content).

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SemanticExtensions.SemanticListLabel(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Marks the label of a list item. This container holds the bullet, number (e.g., '1.'), or term (in a definition list) that identifies the list item.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SemanticExtensions.SemanticListItemBody(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Marks the body or descriptive content of a . This contains the main text or content associated with the list item's label.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SemanticExtensions.SemanticTable(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Marks a container as a table. The library automatically automatically tags headers, rows, cells, etc.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SemanticExtensions.SemanticSpan(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.String)
member.summary: Marks a generic inline portion of text (Span). This is useful for grouping inline elements or applying styling, similar to an HTML <span>.
member.param: Optional alternative text, often used to provide an expansion for an abbreviation or other supplementary information.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SemanticExtensions.SemanticQuote(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Marks an inline portion of text as a quote. This differs from , which is intended for block-level content (one or more paragraphs).

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SemanticExtensions.SemanticCode(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Marks a fragment of text as computer code.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SemanticExtensions.SemanticLink(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.String)
member.summary: Marks the content as a hyperlink (Link).
member.param: Alternative text describing the link's purpose or destination. This is essential for screen readers.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SemanticExtensions.SemanticFigure(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.String)
member.summary: Marks a container as a figure, which is an item of graphical content like a chart, diagram, or photograph.
member.param: A textual description of the figure, read by screen readers. This is essential for accessibility.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SemanticExtensions.SemanticImage(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.String)
member.summary: An alias for . Marks the content as an image.
member.param: A textual description of the image, read by screen readers. This is essential for accessibility.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SemanticExtensions.SemanticFormula(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.String)
member.summary: Marks the content as a mathematical formula. From a structural and accessibility standpoint, it is treated similarly to a figure.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.ShrinkExtensions.Shrink(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary:
Renders its content in the most compact size achievable. Ideal for situations where the parent element provides more space than necessary.
Learn more

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.ShrinkExtensions.ShrinkVertical(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary:
Minimizes content height to the minimum, optimizing vertical space. Ideal for situations where the parent element provides more space than necessary.
Learn more

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.ShrinkExtensions.ShrinkHorizontal(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary:
Minimizes content width to the minimum, optimizing horizontal space. Ideal for situations where the parent element provides more space than necessary.
Learn more

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.StyledBoxExtensions.Border(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.Single,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Color)
member.summary: Sets a uniform border around the container with the specified thickness and color.
member.param: Color in a valid format (like #FF8800 for orange) or from the predefined set (like Colors.Red.Lighten1).

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.StyledBoxExtensions.Background(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Color)
member.summary: Sets a solid background color behind its content. Learn more
member.param: Color in a valid format (like #FF8800 for orange) or from the predefined set (like Colors.Red.Lighten1).

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.StyledBoxExtensions.BackgroundLinearGradient(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.Single,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Color[])
member.summary: Applies a linear gradient background to the container with the specified angle and colors.
member.param: The angle in degrees of the gradient direction.
An array of representing the gradient colors.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.StyledBoxExtensions.Border(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.Single,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Unit)
member.summary: Sets a uniform border (all edges) for its content. Learn more

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.StyledBoxExtensions.BorderVertical(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.Single,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Unit)
member.summary: Sets a vertical border (left and right) for its content. Learn more

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.StyledBoxExtensions.BorderHorizontal(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.Single,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Unit)
member.summary: Sets a horizontal border (top and bottom) for its content. Learn more

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.StyledBoxExtensions.BorderLeft(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.Single,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Unit)
member.summary: Sets a border on the left side of its content. Learn more

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.StyledBoxExtensions.BorderRight(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.Single,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Unit)
member.summary: Sets a border on the right side of its content. Learn more

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.StyledBoxExtensions.BorderTop(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.Single,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Unit)
member.summary: Sets a border on the top side of its content. Learn more

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.StyledBoxExtensions.BorderBottom(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.Single,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Unit)
member.summary: Sets a border on the bottom side of its content. Learn more

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.StyledBoxExtensions.CornerRadius(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.Single,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Unit)
member.summary: Applies a uniform corner radius to all corners of the container with the specified value and unit.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.StyledBoxExtensions.CornerRadiusTopLeft(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.Single,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Unit)
member.summary: Applies a corner radius to the top-left corner of the container with the specified value and unit.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.StyledBoxExtensions.CornerRadiusTopRight(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.Single,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Unit)
member.summary: Applies a corner radius to the top-right corner of the container with the specified value and unit.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.StyledBoxExtensions.CornerRadiusBottomLeft(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.Single,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Unit)
member.summary: Applies a border radius to the bottom-left corner of the container with the specified value and unit.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.StyledBoxExtensions.CornerRadiusBottomRight(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.Single,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Unit)
member.summary: Applies a corner radius to the bottom-right corner of the container with the specified value and unit.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.StyledBoxExtensions.BorderColor(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Color)
member.summary: Adjusts color of the border element. Learn more
member.param: Color in a valid format (like #FF8800 for orange) or from the predefined set (like Colors.Red.Lighten1).

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.StyledBoxExtensions.BorderLinearGradient(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.Single,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Color[])
member.summary: Sets a linear gradient for the border with the specified angle and colors.
member.param: The angle of the gradient in degrees.
An array of objects representing the gradient colors.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.StyledBoxExtensions.BorderAlignmentOutside(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Aligns the container's border to the outer edge of the element.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.StyledBoxExtensions.BorderAlignmentMiddle(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Aligns the border in the middle of the specified container boundaries. This option is used by default when no alignment is specified.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.StyledBoxExtensions.BorderAlignmentInside(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Aligns the border to the inside of the container.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.StyledBoxExtensions.Shadow(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.BoxShadowStyle)
member.summary: Applies a shadow to the container using the specified shadow style. Shadows can enhance the visual depth and separation of elements in a document.

member.name: T:QuestPDF.Fluent.GenerateDynamicSvgDelegate
member.summary: Generates an SVG image based on the given resolution.
member.param: Desired resolution of the image in pixels.
member.returns: An SVG format compatible text.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SvgImageDescriptor.FitWidth
member.summary: Scales the image to fill the full width of its container. This is the default behavior.

summary.para: The image stretches vertically to fit the full available height.
Often used with height-constraining elements such as: Height, MaxHeight, etc.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SvgImageDescriptor.FitArea
member.summary: Combines the FitWidth and FitHeight settings. The image resizes itself to utilize all available space, preserving its aspect ratio. It will either fill the width or height based on the container's dimensions.
member.remarks: An optimal and safe choice.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SvgExtensions.Svg(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.String)
member.summary: Draws the SVG image loaded from a text. Learn more
member.remarks: Multiple strategies exist for the Image element to determine its final size. By default, the image fills all the available width. This behavior can be customized using options within the descriptor class.
member.param: Either a path to the SVG file or the SVG content itself.
member.returns: Descriptor allowing adjustments to the SVG image scaling behavior.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SvgExtensions.Svg(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.SvgImage)
member.summary: Draws the object. Allows to optimize the generation process. Learn more
member.remarks: Multiple strategies exist for the Image element to determine its final size. By default, the image fills all the available width. This behavior can be customized using options within the descriptor class.
member.returns: Descriptor allowing adjustments to the SVG image scaling behavior.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.SvgExtensions.Svg(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,QuestPDF.Fluent.GenerateDynamicSvgDelegate)
member.summary: Renders an SVG image of dynamic size dictated by the document layout constraints.
member.remarks: Ideal for integrating with other libraries, e.g. SkiaSharp or ScottPlot.
member.param: A delegate that requests an image of desired size.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TableColumnsDefinitionDescriptor.ConstantColumn(System.Single,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Unit)
member.summary: Defines a column of constant size that occupies the specified horizontal space.
member.returns: The container of the newly created column.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TableColumnsDefinitionDescriptor.RelativeColumn(System.Single)
member.summary: Defines a column with a relative size that adjusts its width in relation to other relative columns. Learn more
member.example: For a table 100 points wide with three columns: a relative size of 1, a relative size of 5, and a constant size of 10 points, they will span 15 points, 75 points, and 10 points respectively.
member.returns: The container for the newly defined column.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TableCellDescriptor.Cell
member.summary: Inserts a new item into the table element.
member.returns: The container for the newly inserted cell. Provides options to adjust the cell's position, size, and content.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TableDescriptor.ColumnsDefinition(System.Action{QuestPDF.Fluent.TableColumnsDefinitionDescriptor})
member.summary: Specifies the order and size of the table columns. Learn more
member.remarks: This configuration affects both the main content as well as the header and footer sections.
member.param: Handler to define columns of the table.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TableDescriptor.ExtendLastCellsToTableBottom
member.summary: Adjusts rendering algorithm to better handle complex table structures, especially those spanning multiple pages. This applies a unique rule to the final cells in each column, ensuring they stretch to fill the table's bottom edge. Such an approach can enhance your table's visual appeal.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TableDescriptor.Header(System.Action{QuestPDF.Fluent.TableCellDescriptor})
member.summary: Specifies a table header that appears on each page, positioned above the main content. The cell placement and dimensions in this header are distinct from those in the main content. Learn more
member.param: Handler for configuring the header cells.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TableDescriptor.Footer(System.Action{QuestPDF.Fluent.TableCellDescriptor})
member.summary: Specifies a table footer that appears on each page, positioned below the main content. The placement and dimensions of cells within this footer are distinct from the main content. Learn more

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TableDescriptor.Cell
member.summary: Inserts a new item into the table element.
member.returns: The container for the newly inserted cell, enabling customization of its position, size, and content.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TableExtensions.Table(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.Action{QuestPDF.Fluent.TableDescriptor})

summary.para: Renders a set of items utilizing the table layout algorithm.
Items may be auto-placed based on the order they're called or can have assigned specific column and row positions. Cells can also span multiple columns and/or rows.

member.param: Handler to define the table content.

member.summary: Specifies the column position (horizontal axis) of the cell. Learn more
member.param: Columns are numbered starting with 1.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TableCellExtensions.ColumnSpan(QuestPDF.Elements.Table.ITableCellContainer,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Defines the number of columns a cell spans in the horizontal axis. Learn more
member.remarks: Useful when creating complex layouts.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TableCellExtensions.Row(QuestPDF.Elements.Table.ITableCellContainer,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Specifies the row position (vertical axis) of the cell. Learn more
member.param: Rows are numbered starting with 1.

member.summary: Defines the number of rows a cell spans in the vertical axis. Learn more
member.remarks: Useful when creating complex layouts.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TableCellExtensions.AsSemanticHorizontalHeader(QuestPDF.Elements.Table.ITableCellContainer)
member.summary: Marks the specified table cell as a semantic horizontal header. This allows assistive technologies to recognize the cell as a header, improving accessibility and semantic structure.

member.name: T:QuestPDF.Fluent.PageNumberFormatter
member.summary: Transforms a page number into a custom text format (e.g., roman numerals).

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextPageNumberDescriptor.Format(QuestPDF.Fluent.PageNumberFormatter)
member.summary: Provides the capability to render the page number in a custom text format (e.g., roman numerals). Lear more
member.param: The function designated to modify the number into text. When given a null input, a typical-sized placeholder text must be produced.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextBlockDescriptor.AlignLeft
member.summary: Aligns text horizontally to the left side.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextBlockDescriptor.AlignCenter
member.summary: Aligns text horizontally to the center, ensuring equal space on both left and right sides.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextBlockDescriptor.AlignRight
member.summary: Aligns content horizontally to the right side.

summary.para: Justifies the text within its container.

This method sets the horizontal alignment of the text to be justified, meaning it aligns along both the left and right margins. This is achieved by adjusting the spacing between words and characters as necessary so that each line of text stretches from one end of the text column to the other. This creates a clean, block-like appearance for the text.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextBlockDescriptor.AlignStart
member.summary: Aligns the text horizontally to the start of the container. This method sets the horizontal alignment of the text to the start (left for left-to-right languages, right for right-to-left languages).

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextBlockDescriptor.AlignEnd
member.summary: Aligns the text horizontally to the end of the container. This method sets the horizontal alignment of the text to the end (right for left-to-right languages, left for right-to-left languages).

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextBlockDescriptor.ClampLines(System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Limits the number of visible lines in a paragraph, truncating overflow text with an ellipsis or by hiding it to maintain layout consistency.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextBlockDescriptor.ParagraphSpacing(System.Single,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Unit)
member.summary: Adjusts the vertical gap between successive paragraphs (separated by line breaks), helping to visually separate blocks of text for improved readability.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextBlockDescriptor.ParagraphFirstLineIndentation(System.Single,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Unit)
member.summary: Specifies the horizontal offset of the first line in a paragraph. Commonly used to visually separate paragraphs in a block of text.

This method sets the horizontal alignment of the text to be justified, meaning it aligns along both the left and right margins. This is achieved by adjusting the spacing between words and characters as necessary so that each line of text stretches from one end of the text column to the other. member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextDescriptor.PageNumberWithinSection(System.String)
member.summary: Appends text showing the page number relative to the beginning of the given Section.
member.example: For a section spanning pages 20 to 50, page 35 will show as 15.
member.param: The text key corresponding to the value used when defining the Section element.

returns.para: Handler for adjusting the appearance of the text span, including attributes like color, size, and background.
Additionally, it provides formatting options, like displaying the page number in custom text format (e.g. Roman numerals).

member.summary: Appends text showing the total number of pages within the given Section.
member.example: For a section spanning pages 20 to 50, the total is 30 pages.

Additionally, it provides formatting options, like displaying the page number in custom text format (e.g. Roman numerals).

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextSpanDescriptorExtensions.Style``1(``0,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.TextStyle)
member.summary: Sets a font style using the typography pattern. Learn more
member.remarks: This API reduces the garbage collector pressure and offers the best performance.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextSpanDescriptorExtensions.FontColor``1(``0,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Color)
member.summary: Sets the font color. The font color determines the color applied to text characters, affecting their visual appearance.
member.remarks: It also influences the default color of text decorations, such as underlines.
member.param: Color in a valid format (like #FF8800 for orange) or from the predefined set (like Colors.Red.Lighten1).

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextSpanDescriptorExtensions.BackgroundColor``1(``0,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Color)
member.summary: Sets a solid background color for the text. This color fills the area behind the text or other elements, enhancing contrast and providing visual emphasis.
member.param: Color in a valid format (like #FF8800 for orange) or from the predefined set (like Colors.Red.Lighten1).

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextSpanDescriptorExtensions.FontFamily``1(``0,System.String[])
member.summary: Sets the font family of the text. A font family is a collection of related fonts that share a consistent design style but may vary in weight, style, or width. Examples of font families include Arial, Times New Roman, and Calibri.

remarks.para: By default, the library searches all fonts available in the runtime environment.
This may work well on the development environment but may fail in the cloud where fonts are usually not installed.
It is safest deploy font files along with the application and then register them using the FontManager class.

member.param: Actual font family (e.g. "Times New Roman", "Calibri", "Lato") or custom identifier used when invoking the FontManager method.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextSpanDescriptorExtensions.FontSize``1(``0,System.Single)
member.summary: Sets font size for the text. Font size measures the height of text characters, determining how large or small the text appears.
member.remarks: It's worth noting that different fonts may render text with distinct visual sizes, even when assigned the same numerical font size.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextSpanDescriptorExtensions.LineHeight``1(``0,System.Nullable{System.Single})
member.summary: Adjusts the vertical spacing between lines of text, affecting readability and overall text layout. The added space is proportional to the text size. A value of 1 retains the original spacing. Learn more

param.name: factor
param.para: Sets the proportion of vertical spacing relative to the font size.
A value greater than 1 increases the spacing, while a value less than 1 decreases it. The value must be greater than 0.
A value of 1 sets the line height equal to the font size. This setting may result in insufficient vertical spacing around the text.
A value of default indicates that the font metrics should be used to calculate the line spacing, which is the default behavior.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextSpanDescriptorExtensions.LetterSpacing``1(``0,System.Single)
member.summary: Adjusts the horizontal spacing between characters in the text, affecting readability and overall visual style. The adjustment is proportional to the text size. Learn more

param.name: factor
param.para: Sets the proportion by which the horizontal space between characters is changed.
A value of 0 maintains the original spacing. When the value is positive, the text is more spread out. When it is negative, the text is more condensed.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextSpanDescriptorExtensions.WordSpacing``1(``0,System.Single)
member.summary: Adjusts the horizontal spacing between words in the text, affecting readability and overall visual style. The adjustment is proportional to the text size.

param.name: factor
param.para: Sets the proportion by which the horizontal space between words is changed.
A value of 0 maintains the original spacing. When the value is positive, the text is more spread out. When it is negative, the text is more condensed.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextSpanDescriptorExtensions.Italic``1(``0,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Renders text with an italic effect, where letters are slightly slanted to the right. Commonly used for emphasis or to distinguish specific words.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextSpanDescriptorExtensions.WrapAnywhere``1(``0,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Allows text to wrap at any character, not just spaces.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextSpanDescriptorExtensions.Strikethrough``1(``0,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Draws a line through the middle of the text.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextSpanDescriptorExtensions.Underline``1(``0,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Draws a line under the text.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextSpanDescriptorExtensions.Overline``1(``0,System.Boolean)
