member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextSpanDescriptorExtensions.EnableFontFeature``1(``0,System.String)

summary.para: Enables or disables font features defined by the OpenType standard, e.g. kernig, ligatures.

Font features are always encoded as 4-character long strings. For example, the ligatures feature is encode as liga, while the kernig feature as kern. For a list of available features, refer to the FontFeatures class.

remarks.para: To better understand what font features are, please consider example definitions. Please note that there are many more various font features. Most fonts support only a handful of them, having some of them enabled by default:

Ligatures in typography are specific character combinations that are designed to improve the aesthetics and readability of certain letter pairs. For example, in some fonts, when you type certain combinations of letters like 'fi' or 'fl', they will be replaced with a single, joined glyph.

Kerning in typography refers to the adjustment of space between characters in a proportional font. It's used to achieve a visually pleasing result by adjusting the spacing of specific character pairs. For example, in many fonts, the pair 'AV' is kerned so that the 'A' and 'V' are closer together than they would be by default.

example.para: TextStyle.Default.EnableFontFeature(FontFeatures.StandardLigatures);
TextStyle.Default.DisableFontFeature(FontFeatures.Kerning);

member.param: Provide font feature name or use the FontFeatures class.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextSpanDescriptorExtensions.DisableFontFeature``1(``0,System.String)

summary.para: Enables or disables font features defined by the OpenType standard, e.g. kernig, ligatures.

Font features are always encoded as 4-character long strings. For example, the ligatures feature is encode as liga, while the kernig feature as kern. For a list of available features, refer to the FontFeatures class.

remarks.para: To better understand what font features are, please consider example definitions. Please note that there are many more various font features. Most fonts support only a handful of them, having some of them enabled by default:

Ligatures in typography are specific character combinations that are designed to improve the aesthetics and readability of certain letter pairs. For example, in some fonts, when you type certain combinations of letters like 'fi' or 'fl', they will be replaced with a single, joined glyph.

Kerning in typography refers to the adjustment of space between characters in a proportional font. It's used to achieve a visually pleasing result by adjusting the spacing of specific character pairs. For example, in many fonts, the pair 'AV' is kerned so that the 'A' and 'V' are closer together than they would be by default.

example.para: TextStyle.Default.EnableFontFeature(FontFeatures.StandardLigatures);
TextStyle.Default.DisableFontFeature(FontFeatures.Kerning);

member.param: Provide font feature name or use the FontFeatures class.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextStyleExtensions.FontColor(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.TextStyle,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Color)
member.summary: Sets the font color. The font color determines the color applied to text characters, affecting their visual appearance.
member.remarks: It also influences the default color of text decorations, such as underlines.
member.param: Color in a valid format (like #FF8800 for orange) or from the predefined set (like Colors.Red.Lighten1).

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextStyleExtensions.BackgroundColor(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.TextStyle,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Color)
member.summary: Sets a solid background color for the text. This color fills the area behind the text or other elements, enhancing contrast and providing visual emphasis.
member.param: Color in a valid format (like #FF8800 for orange) or from the predefined set (like Colors.Red.Lighten1).

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextStyleExtensions.FontFamily(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.TextStyle,System.String[])
member.summary: Sets the font family of the text. A font family is a collection of related fonts that share a consistent design style but may vary in weight, style, or width. Examples of font families include Arial, Times New Roman, and Calibri.

remarks.para: By default, the library searches all fonts available in the runtime environment.
This may work well on the development environment but may fail in the cloud where fonts are usually not installed.
It is safest deploy font files along with the application and then register them using the FontManager class.

member.param: Actual font family (e.g. "Times New Roman", "Calibri", "Lato") or custom identifier used when invoking the FontManager method.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextStyleExtensions.FontSize(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.TextStyle,System.Single)
member.summary: Sets font size for the text. Font size measures the height of text characters, determining how large or small the text appears.
member.remarks: It's worth noting that different fonts may render text with distinct visual sizes, even when assigned the same numerical font size.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextStyleExtensions.LineHeight(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.TextStyle,System.Nullable{System.Single})
member.summary: Adjusts the vertical spacing between lines of text, affecting readability and overall text layout. The added space is proportional to the text size. A value of 1 retains the original spacing. Learn more

param.name: factor
param.para: Sets the proportion of vertical spacing relative to the font size.
A value greater than 1 increases the spacing, while a value less than 1 decreases it. The value must be greater than 0.
A value of 1 sets the line height equal to the font size. This setting may result in insufficient vertical spacing around the text.
A value of default indicates that the font metrics should be used to calculate the line spacing, which is the default behavior.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextStyleExtensions.LetterSpacing(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.TextStyle,System.Single)
member.summary: Adjusts the horizontal spacing between characters in the text, affecting readability and overall visual style. The adjustment is proportional to the text size. Learn more

param.name: factor
param.para: Sets the proportion by which the horizontal space between characters is changed.
A value of 0 maintains the original spacing. When the value is positive, the text is more spread out. When it is negative, the text is more condensed.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextStyleExtensions.WordSpacing(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.TextStyle,System.Single)
member.summary: Adjusts the horizontal spacing between words in the text, affecting readability and overall visual style. The adjustment is proportional to the text size.

param.name: factor
param.para: Sets the proportion by which the horizontal space between words is changed.
A value of 0 maintains the original spacing. When the value is positive, the text is more spread out. When it is negative, the text is more condensed.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextStyleExtensions.Italic(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.TextStyle,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Renders text with an italic effect, where letters are slightly slanted to the right. Commonly used for emphasis or to distinguish specific words.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextStyleExtensions.WrapAnywhere(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.TextStyle,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Allows text to wrap at any character, not just spaces.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextStyleExtensions.Strikethrough(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.TextStyle,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Draws a line through the middle of the text.
member.remarks: You can control the decoration visuals by setting color and style (solid, double, wavy, dotted, dashed) of the line.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextStyleExtensions.Underline(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.TextStyle,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Draws a line under the text.
member.remarks: You can control the decoration visuals by setting color and style (solid, double, wavy, dotted, dashed) of the line.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextStyleExtensions.Overline(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.TextStyle,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Draws a line above the text.
member.remarks: You can control the decoration visuals by setting color and style (solid, double, wavy, dotted, dashed) of the line.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextStyleExtensions.DecorationColor(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.TextStyle,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Color)
member.summary: Sets the color of the text decoration.
member.remarks: Applies for the following text decorations: striketrough, underline, overline.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextStyleExtensions.DecorationThickness(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.TextStyle,System.Single)

member.remarks: Applies for the following text decorations: striketrough, underline, overline.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextStyleExtensions.DecorationSolid(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.TextStyle)

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextStyleExtensions.DecorationDouble(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.TextStyle)

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextStyleExtensions.DecorationWavy(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.TextStyle)

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextStyleExtensions.DecorationDotted(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.TextStyle)

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextStyleExtensions.DecorationDashed(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.TextStyle)

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TextStyleExtensions.Thin(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.TextStyle)
member.summary: Sets the font weight to "thin" (equivalent to CSS 100). Learn more

remarks.para: Determines the thickness of the text characters, ranging from light to bold, to create visual hierarchy or emphasis.
Not all fonts support every weight. If the specified weight isn't available, the library selects the closest available option.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TranslateExtensions.TranslateX(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.Single,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Unit)
member.summary:
Moves content along the horizontal axis. A positive value moves content to the right; a negative value moves it to the left. Does not alter the available space.
Learn more

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Fluent.TranslateExtensions.TranslateY(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer,System.Single,QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Unit)
member.summary:
Moves content along the vertical axis. A positive value moves content downwards, a negative value moves it upwards. Does not alter the available space.
Learn more

member.name: T:QuestPDF.Helpers.Colors
member.summary:
Offers a palette of colors defined by the Google Material Design System guidelines. Each primary color (like red, blue, green) has associated shades and accent variations.
Learn more

member.name: F:QuestPDF.Helpers.FontFeatures.HojoKanjiForms
member.summary: (JIS X 0212-1990 Kanji Forms)

member.name: T:QuestPDF.Helpers.Fonts
member.summary: Contains a collection of fonts defined by the PDF standard.

member.name: T:QuestPDF.Helpers.PageSize
member.summary: Defines the physical dimensions (width and height) of a page.

remarks.para: Commonly used page sizes are available in the class.
Change page orientation with the Portrait and Landscape extension methods.

member.name: T:QuestPDF.Helpers.PageSizes
member.summary: Contains a collection of predefined, common and standard page sizes, such as A4 with dimensions of 595 points in width and 842 points in height.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Helpers.PageSizeExtensions.Portrait(QuestPDF.Helpers.PageSize)
member.summary: Sets page size to a portrait orientation, making the width smaller than the height.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Helpers.PageSizeExtensions.Landscape(QuestPDF.Helpers.PageSize)
member.summary: Sets page size to a landscape orientation, making the width bigger than the height.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Helpers.Placeholders.LoremIpsum
member.summary: Returns the commonly used 'Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet' placeholder text.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Helpers.Placeholders.Label
member.summary: Generates a random text ideal for concise labels like product names.
member.example:
"Beatae dolor"
"Rerum quibusdam perspiciatis"
"Fugiat aperiam officiis"

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Helpers.Placeholders.Sentence
member.summary: Generates random text ideal for single sentences, like product description.
member.example: Vero a id optio consequuntur dignissimos repellendus provident blanditiis.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Helpers.Placeholders.Question
member.summary: Generates random text formatted as a question.
member.example: Sequi enim voluptas quasi modi aspernatur dolorem?

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Helpers.Placeholders.Paragraph
member.summary: Generates random text suited for paragraphs, like detailed product description.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Helpers.Placeholders.Paragraphs
member.summary: Generates random text ideal for multiple paragraphs, resembling an article.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Helpers.Placeholders.Email
member.summary: Generates random text in the format of an email address.
member.example: consequuntur35@blanditiis.com

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Helpers.Placeholders.Name
member.summary: Generates random text looking like a two-word name, with capitalized initials.
member.example:
"Voluptates Inventore"
"Praesentium Consectetur"
"Voluptatibus Molestias"

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Helpers.Placeholders.PhoneNumber
member.summary: Generates random text in the format of a phone number.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Helpers.Placeholders.WebpageUrl
member.summary: Generates random text resembling a webpage address.
member.example: www.libero.com

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Helpers.Placeholders.Price
member.summary: Generates random text resembling a price value.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Helpers.Placeholders.Time
member.summary: Generates random text representation of a random time.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Helpers.Placeholders.ShortDate
member.summary: Generates random text that resembles a date value using short formatting.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Helpers.Placeholders.LongDate
member.summary: Generates random text that resembles a full date value.
member.example: Monday, 18 November 2024

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Helpers.Placeholders.DateTime
member.summary: Generates random text that resembles a datetime value.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Helpers.Placeholders.Integer
member.summary: Generates random text mimicking an integer value, ranging from 0 to 10,000.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Helpers.Placeholders.Decimal
member.summary: Generates random text in the style of a local-formatted decimal, values from 0 to 100 with two decimal points precision.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Helpers.Placeholders.Percent
member.summary: Generates random text resembling a percentage value.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Helpers.Placeholders.BackgroundColor
member.summary: Returns a random bright color from the Material Design palette.
member.example:
#ffab91
#bcaaa4
#ffab91

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Helpers.Placeholders.Color
member.summary: Returns a random color from the Material Design palette.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Helpers.Placeholders.Image(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Generates a random image with a soft color gradient with provided and .
member.remarks: Caution: using this method may significantly reduce document generation performance. Please do not use it when performing benchmarks.
member.returns: Random image encoded in the JPEG format.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Helpers.Placeholders.Image(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.ImageSize)
member.summary: Generates a random image with a soft color gradient.
member.remarks: For performance reasons, this method may reduce the argument to at most 64 pixels, while preserving its aspect ratio.
member.returns: Random image encoded in the JPEG format.

member.name: F:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.AspectRatioOption.FitWidth
member.summary: Adjusts content to occupy the full width available.
member.remarks: Used as the default setting in the library.

member.name: F:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.AspectRatioOption.FitHeight
member.summary: Adjusts content to fill the available height.
member.remarks: Often used with height-constraining elements such as: Height, MaxHeight, etc.

member.name: F:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.AspectRatioOption.FitArea
member.summary: Adjusts content to fill the available area while maintaining its aspect ratio. This may result in the content fully occupying either the width or height, depending on its dimensions.
member.remarks: Often used with constraining elements such as: Width, MaxWidth, Height, MaxHeight, etc.

member.name: T:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.BoxShadowStyle
member.summary: Represents the visual styling properties for a box shadow effect.

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.BoxShadowStyle.OffsetX
member.summary: Gets or sets the horizontal offset of the shadow in pixels. Positive values move the shadow to the right, negative values move it to the left.

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.BoxShadowStyle.OffsetY
member.summary: Gets or sets the vertical offset of the shadow in pixels. Positive values move the shadow downward, negative values move it upward.

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.BoxShadowStyle.Spread
member.summary: Gets or sets the spread radius of the shadow in pixels. Positive values cause the shadow to expand, negative values cause it to contract.

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.BoxShadowStyle.Blur
member.summary: Gets or sets the blur radius of the shadow in pixels. Higher values produce a more diffused shadow with softer edges. A value of 0 results in a sharp, unblurred shadow.
member.remarks: Values different from 0 may significantly impact performance and enlarge the output file size. Use with caution, especially in large documents or when rendering complex shadows.

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.BoxShadowStyle.Color
member.summary: Gets or sets the color of the shadow.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Color.#ctor(System.UInt32)
member.summary: Creates a new color from a hex value following the ARGB format. For example 0xFF0000FF represents a fully opaque blue color.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Color.WithAlpha(System.Byte)
member.summary: Creates a new color instance with the specified alpha transparency value. The alpha value should be within the range 0 to 255, where 0 represents fully transparent and 255 represents fully opaque.
member.returns: A new color instance with the adjusted alpha transparency.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Color.WithAlpha(System.Single)
member.summary: Creates a new color instance with the specified alpha transparency value. The alpha value should be within the range 0 to 1, where 0 represents fully transparent and 1 represents fully opaque.
member.returns: A new color instance with the adjusted alpha transparency.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Color.FromHex(System.String)
member.summary: Creates a new color instance from a hex string representation. The string should follow the formats: #RGB, #ARGB, #RRGGBB, or #AARRGGBB. The hash sign is optional; for example: RGB, ARGB, RRGGBB, or AARRGGBB are also valid.
member.param: The hex string representing the color.
member.returns: A new color instance created from the hex string.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Color.FromRGB(System.Byte,System.Byte,System.Byte)
member.summary: Creates a new color instance given the red, green, and blue component values. Each component should be within the range 0 to 255.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Color.FromARGB(System.Byte,System.Byte,System.Byte,System.Byte)
member.summary: Creates a new color instance from the specified alpha, red, green, and blue component values. Each component should be within the range 0 to 255.

member.name: F:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.ContentDirection.LeftToRight
member.summary: Sets the left-to-right (LTR) content direction. Learn more

remarks.para: This writing system is used by most of modern languages.

The content direction affects various layout structures. In LTR mode, items are typically aligned to the left. This mode also influences the direction of items in certain layouts. For instance, in a row element with LTR mode, the first item is positioned on the left, while the last item is on the right.

member.name: F:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.ContentDirection.RightToLeft
member.summary: Sets the right-to-left (RTL) content direction. Learn more

remarks.para: This writing system is used by languages such as Hebrew, Arabic, and Persian.

The content direction affects various layout structures. In RTL mode, items are typically aligned to the right. This mode also influences the direction of items in certain layouts. For instance, in a row element with RTL mode, the first item is positioned on the right, while the last item is on the left.

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.DocumentMetadata.Title
member.summary: Represents the main heading or name of the document, often displayed as a prominent identifier or label in PDF metadata.

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.DocumentMetadata.Author
member.summary: Specifies the individual or entity responsible for creating the document.

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.DocumentMetadata.Subject
member.summary: Provides a brief description or main topic related to the document content.

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.DocumentMetadata.Keywords
member.summary: Defines a collection of terms or phrases that describe the document's content or purpose. Improves categorization and searchability of the document.

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.DocumentMetadata.Creator
member.summary: Identifies the software or system that generated the document.

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.DocumentMetadata.Producer
member.summary: Specifies the name of the application or library that generated the document.

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.DocumentMetadata.Language
member.summary: Specifies the language of the document content, defined using language tags such as "en-US" for American English.

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.DocumentMetadata.CreationDate
member.summary: Represents the date and time when the document was created. This property is used to specify the creation timestamp.

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.DocumentMetadata.ModifiedDate
member.summary: Stores the most recent date and time when the content or metadata of the document was updated. It is used to provide metadata information about the last revision of the document.

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.DocumentSettings.PDFA_Conformance
member.summary: Gets or sets the PDF/A conformance level for the document. This property determines the adherence of the generated PDF to specific archival standards.

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.DocumentSettings.PDFUA_Conformance
member.summary: Gets or sets the conformance level for PDF/UA (Universal Accessibility) compliance. Warning: this setting makes the document non-reproducable.

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.DocumentSettings.CompressDocument
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the generated document should be additionally compressed. May greatly reduce file size with a small increase in generation time.

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.DocumentSettings.ImageCompressionQuality
member.summary: Encoding quality controls the trade-off between size and quality. When the image is opaque, it will be encoded using the JPEG format with the selected quality setting. When the image contains an alpha channel, it is always encoded using the PNG format and this option is ignored. The default value is "high quality".
member.remarks: This setting is taken into account only when the image is in the JPG format, otherwise it is ignored.

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.DocumentSettings.ImageRasterDpi
member.summary: The DPI (pixels-per-inch) at which images and features without native PDF support will be rasterized. A larger DPI would create a PDF that reflects the original intent with better fidelity, but it can make for larger PDF files too, which would use more memory while rendering, and it would be slower to be processed or sent online or to printer. When generating images, this parameter also controls the resolution of the generated content. Default value is 288.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.EmptyContainer.Create
member.summary: Creates an empty IContainer instance.

member.name: T:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IComponent

summary.para: This interface represents a reusable document fragment.

Components serve as modular building blocks for abstracting document layouts. They promote code reusability across multiple sections or types of documents. Using a component, you can generate content based on its internal state.

member.example: Consider the scenario of a company-wide page header. Instead of replicating the same header design across various documents, a single component can be created and referenced wherever needed.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IComponent.Compose(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer)
member.summary: Method invoked by the library to compose document content.

member.name: T:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IContainer
member.summary: Represents a layout structure with exactly one child element.
member.remarks: The main purpose of this interface is to facilitate the Fluent API's construction. It's not intended to allow external creation of new container kinds or layout designs.

member.name: T:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IDocument
member.summary: Represents the document abstraction, including its content, metadata, and configuration settings. Learn more

remarks.para: Implement this interface to centralize your entire document's structure within a single class for easy management.
For a different approach, consider the Minimal API pathway.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IDocument.Compose(QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IDocumentContainer)
member.summary: Configures the document content by specifying its layout structure and visual element.
member.param: The document container used for defining content via the FluentAPI.

member.name: T:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IDocumentContainer
member.summary: Represents the primary container encapsulating the entire content of the document.

member.name: T:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.DynamicComponentComposeResult
member.summary: Represents the output from the DynamicComponent describing what should be rendered on the current page.

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.DynamicComponentComposeResult.Content
member.summary: Any content created with the method that should be drawn on the currently rendered page.

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.DynamicComponentComposeResult.HasMoreContent
member.summary: Set to true if the dynamic component has additional content for the next page. Set to false if all content from the dynamic component has been rendered.

member.name: T:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IDynamicComponent
member.summary: Represents a dynamically generated section of the document. Components are page-aware, understand their positioning, can dynamically construct other content elements, and assess their dimensions, enabling complex layout creations.
member.remarks: Though dynamic components offer great flexibility, be cautious of potential performance impacts.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IDynamicComponent.Compose(QuestPDF.Elements.DynamicContext)
member.summary: Method invoked by the library to plan and create new content for each page.
member.remarks: Remember, the QuestPDF library can invoke the Compose method more than once for each page and might adjust the state internally.
member.param: Context offering additional information (like current page number, entire document size) and the capability to produce dynamic content elements.
member.returns: Representation of content that should be placed on the current page.

member.name: T:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IDynamicComponent`1
member.summary: Represents a section of the document dynamically created based on its inner state. Components are page-aware, understand their positioning, can dynamically construct other content elements, and assess their dimensions, enabling complex layout creations.
member.remarks: Though dynamic components offer great flexibility, be cautious of potential performance impacts.
member.typeparam: Structure type representing the internal state of the component.

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IDynamicComponent`1.State
member.summary: Represents the component's current state.

remarks.para: The state should remain read-only. Avoid direct state modifications. For any alterations, generate a new struct instance and reassign the State property.
Remember, the QuestPDF library can invoke the Compose method more than once for each page and might adjust the state internally.

member.name: T:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.IElement
member.summary:
Represents an element within the document content, for example:
- visual elements (text, image, canvas),
- positional elements (padding, aspect ratio),
- flow-control elements (page break, conditional displays),
- layout elements (table, column),
- and other element types (section, hyperlink, content direction).

member.name: T:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Image

summary.para: Caches the image in local memory for efficient reuse.
Optimizes the generation process, especially:
- For images repeated in a single document to enhance performance and reduce output file size (e.g., an image used as list bullet icon).
- When an image appears on multiple document types for increased generation performance (e.g., a company logo).

member.remarks: This class is thread safe.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Image.FromBinaryData(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Loads the image from binary data. Learn more

remarks.para: Please note that there is a significant difference between image resolution (number of pixels vertically and horizontally) and its physical size described in points. Therefore, the resolution of an image is not used for determining its physical size on the document.

Multiple strategies exist for the Image element to determine its final size. By default, the image fills all the available width. This behavior can be customized using options within the descriptor class.
Images are automatically resized and compressed based on the descriptor's setup. It defaults to global document's settings.
Supported formats: PNG, JPEG and WEBP.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Image.FromFile(System.String)
member.summary: Loads the image from a file with specified path. Learn more

Multiple strategies exist for the Image element to determine its final size. By default, the image fills all the available width. This behavior can be customized using options within the descriptor class.
Images are automatically resized and compressed based on the descriptor's setup. It defaults to global document's settings.
Supported formats: PNG, JPEG and WEBP.

Multiple strategies exist for the Image element to determine its final size. By default, the image fills all the available width. This behavior can be customized using options within the descriptor class.
Images are automatically resized and compressed based on the descriptor's setup. It defaults to global document's settings.
Supported formats: PNG, JPEG and WEBP.

member.summary: JPEG format with target quality set to 100 out of 100

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.ImageGenerationSettings.ImageFormat
member.summary: The file format used to encode the image(s).

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.ImageGenerationSettings.ImageCompressionQuality
member.summary: Encoding quality controls the trade-off between size and quality. The default value is "high".

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.ImageGenerationSettings.RasterDpi
member.summary: The DPI (pixels-per-inch) at which the document will be rasterized. This parameter controls the resolution of produced images. Higher DPI results in superior image quality but may increase the output file size. Default value is 288.
member.example: Consider a document of dimensions 3x4 inches. Using a DPI value of 300, the final image resolution translates to 900x1200 pixels.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.MergedDocument.UseOriginalPageNumbers
member.summary: Documents maintain their own page numbers upon merging, without continuity between them. As a result, APIs related to page numbers reflect individual documents, not the cumulative count. All documents are simply be merged together.
member.example: Merging a two-page document with a three-page document results in a sequence: 1, 2, 1, 2, 3.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.MergedDocument.UseContinuousPageNumbers
member.summary: Consolidates the content from every document, creating a continuous seamless one. Page number APIs return a consecutive numbering for this unified document.
member.example: Merging a two-page document with a three-page document results in a sequence: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5.

member.name: F:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.TextInjectedElementAlignment.AboveBaseline
member.summary: Aligns the bottom edge of the injected element with the text baseline. The injected element sits on top of the baseline.

member.name: F:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.TextInjectedElementAlignment.BelowBaseline
member.summary: Aligns the top edge of the injected element with the text baseline. The injected element hangs below the baseline.

member.name: F:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.TextInjectedElementAlignment.Top
member.summary: Aligns the top edge of the injected element with the top edge of the font. If the injected element is very tall, the extra space will hang from the top and extend through the bottom of the line.

member.name: F:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.TextInjectedElementAlignment.Bottom
member.summary: Aligns the bottom edge of the injected element with the top edge of the font. If the injected element is very tall, the extra space will rise from the bottom and extend through the top of the line.

member.name: F:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.TextInjectedElementAlignment.Middle
member.summary: Aligns the middle of the injected element with the middle of the text. If the injected element is very tall, the extra space will grow equally from the top and bottom of the line.

member.name: F:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Unit.Point
member.summary: Point is a standard unit for all PDF documents. 72 points equal 1 inch

member.name: F:QuestPDF.Infrastructure.Unit.Mil
member.summary: 1/1000th of inch

member.name: T:QuestPDF.Qpdf.SimpleJsonSerializer
member.summary: Never use in performance critical scenarios!

summary.para: Please kindly select license type that applies to your usage of the QuestPDF library.
For more details, please check the QuestPDF License and Pricing webpage

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Settings.EnableCaching
member.summary: This flag generates additional document elements to cache layout calculation results. In the vast majority of cases, this significantly improves performance, while slightly increasing memory consumption.
member.remarks: Enabled by default.

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Settings.EnableDebugging
member.summary: This flag generates additional document elements to improve layout debugging experience. When the provided content contains size constraints impossible to meet, the library generates an enhanced exception message with additional location and layout measurement details.
member.remarks: By default, this flag is enabled only when the debugger IS attached.

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Settings.CheckIfAllTextGlyphsAreAvailable
member.summary: This flag enables checking the font glyph availability. If your text contains glyphs that are not present in the specified font, 1) when this flag is enabled: the DocumentDrawingException is thrown. OR 2) when this flag is disabled: placeholder characters are visible in the produced PDF file. Enabling this flag may slightly decrease document generation performance. However, it provides hints that used fonts are not sufficient to produce correct results.
member.remarks: By default, this flag is enabled only when the debugger IS attached.

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Settings.UseEnvironmentFonts
member.summary: Decides whether the application should use the fonts available in the environment.

remarks.para: When set to true, the application will use the fonts installed on the system where it is running. This is the default behavior.
When set to false, the application will only use the fonts that have been registered using the FontManager class in the QuestPDF library.
This property is useful when you want to control the fonts used by your application, especially in cases where the environment might not have the necessary fonts installed.

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Settings.FontDiscoveryPaths
member.summary: Specifies the collection of paths where the library will automatically search for font files to register.

remarks.para: By default, this collection contains the application files path.
You can add additional paths to this collection to include more directories for automatic font registration.

member.name: P:QuestPDF.Settings.TemporaryStoragePath
member.summary: Gets or sets the file path used for temporary storage during the document generation process. This path is used by various operations that require temporary files.

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Skia.SkCanvas.DrawOverflowArea(QuestPDF.Skia.SkRect)
member.summary: draws stripe pattern (red lines at 45 deegree angle)

member.name: M:QuestPDF.Skia.SkImage.ResizeAndCompress(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Scales image only when target size is smaller than original image size. When image is opaque, uses the JPEG compression algorithm, otherwise uses the PNG algorithm. Only the JPEG compression algorithm uses the compressionQuality parameter.

members.member: Match the baseline of the placeholder with the baseline.
Align the bottom edge of the placeholder with the baseline such that the placeholder sits on top of the baseline.
Align the top edge of the placeholder with the baseline specified in such that the placeholder hangs below the baseline.
Align the top edge of the placeholder with the top edge of the font. When the placeholder is very tall, the extra space will hang from the top and extend through the bottom of the line.
Align the bottom edge of the placeholder with the top edge of the font. When the placeholder is very tall, the extra space will rise from the bottom and extend through the top of the line.
Align the middle of the placeholder with the middle of the text. When the placeholder is very tall, the extra space will grow equally from the top and bottom of the line.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.IsExternalInit
member.summary: Reserved to be used by the compiler for tracking metadata. This class should not be used by developers in source code.
